Title: The Attack on DEI Was a Ruse. We Compared the Qualifications of Obama and Trump Administration Appointees. The ClownBinge Investigation Is Telling.
Tags: DEI, qualifications, Obama, Trump, cabinet appointments, federal appointees, merit, documented record, executive branch
Teaser: The argument against DEI programs has been consistent: they substitute identity for merit, producing less qualified people. That is a testable claim. We tested it. We pulled the academic credentials and professional records of cabinet-level appointees from two administrations, set them side by side, and let the documented record speak. It does.
Full Body Text: This is not opinion. It does not argue one administration was morally superior to another. The word does not appear in the document. Motive is not discussed. The comparison runs department by department, appointee by appointee. It matches credentials and professional experience across both administrations. The evidence is public. Senate confirmation filings. Official biographies. University archives. C-SPAN footage. None of it editorializes. The January 2025 executive orders say it outright: federal DEI programs substitute identity characteristics for merit, placing less qualified people in consequential roles. That can be tested. We tested it. The record is telling. Department of Energy: The Nobel Laureate and the Guy Who Couldn't Remember Its Name Barack Obama appointed Steven Chu as Secretary of Energy in 2009 . Chu held two bachelor's degrees from Rochester: one in Mathematics, one in Physics. Berkeley granted the physics doctorate. He taught at Stanford. Then UC Berkeley. He got the Nobel in physics in 1997. He knew the Department of Energy's function. He had spent decades doing the work it funds. Obama's second Energy Secretary was Ernest Moniz , who held a B.A. from Boston College and a Ph.D. in Theoretical Physics from Stanford. He taught physics at MIT and ran the MIT Energy Initiative, which the university calls one of its major energy policy programs. Donald Trump appointed Rick Perry as Secretary of Energy in 2017 . Perry's degree is in Animal Science from Texas A&M University. He served as Governor of Texas for fourteen years, a legitimate executive credential. He also promised in 2012 to eliminate three federal departments, then forgot which three on live television. He forgot the Department of Energy. He was subsequently confirmed to lead it. The administration that championed DEI produced a Nobel laureate and an MIT theoretical physicist to run the agency responsible for the United States nuclear weapons stockpile and energy research. The administration that declared DEI a threat to merit produced an animal science graduate who forgot the agency existed. Department of Education: Professionals vs. the Brand Deal Arne Duncan, Obama's first Education Secretary, had a sociology degree from Harvard. He ran Chicago Public Schools from 2001 to 2009. The title was CEO. He ran the third-largest school district in the country. King, the second Education Secretary, held three degrees: a B.A. from Harvard, a J.D. from Yale Law, and an Ed.D. from Columbia. King was New York State Education Commissioner. Before that, he taught in public schools. Trump's first Education Secretary was Betsy DeVos , who holds a B.A. in Business Administration and Political Science from Calvin University in Michigan. She did not attend public school. She had not taught in a public school. She had never worked in school administration. She told the Senate during her confirmation hearing that she may have been confused about whether the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act was federal law or state law. IDEA is federal law. Federal law since 1975. The vote was 51-50. Pence cast the deciding vote. That tiebreaker stands alone. No other cabinet nominee in United States history has needed the vice president's vote to confirm. Trump's 2025 Education Secretary was Linda McMahon , who attended East Carolina University without completing a degree. Her primary professional credential is serving as CEO of World Wrestling Entertainment. Trump appointed her to run the Small Business Administration. She did. Her record shows no background in educational policy, curriculum design, special education, pedagogy, or running schools. Department of Housing and Urban Development: A Surgeon and the Housing Experts Obama named Shaun Donovan to run HUD in 2009. Donovan has three Harvard degrees: a B.A., a Master of Architecture, and a Master of Public Administration from the Kennedy School. He ran the Department of Housing Preservation and Development in New York City. Before that, HUD under Clinton. He had spent his career in housing policy. Julian Castro was Obama's second HUD Secretary. Stanford B.A., Harvard J.D. Before that, Mayor of San Antonio. Housing development was a central policy priority there, and urban development issues ran through his career. Trump gave HUD to Ben Carson . Carson went to Yale for undergrad. He got his M.D. from the University of Michigan Medical School. The man can operate. The pediatric neurosurgery record exists. It is on file. His file showed no housing policy background. He had no background in urban development. He had written in his 2015 book A More Perfect Union that subsidized housing creates what he called a form of modern slavery, a claim he made without citation to housing research. He was confirmed to lead the agency that enforces fair housing law, runs public housing, and backs federal mortgages. Environmental Protection Agency: The Litigant Leads the Agency He Sued Jackson ran EPA under Obama. She has a B.S. in Chemical Engineering from Tulane and an M.S. in Chemical Engineering from Princeton. She spent sixteen years at the EPA. Then came the appointment. The job requires expertise in environmental regulation. His second EPA Administrator, Gina McCarthy, held a B.A. in Social Anthropology from the University of Massachusetts Boston and an M.S. in Environmental Health Engineering from Tufts. She worked in environmental policy for thirty-five years. She had served five Republican governors in Massachusetts before her federal appointment. Trump appointed Scott Pruitt as EPA Administrator in 2017 . Pruitt's degrees: a B.A. from Georgetown College, then a J.D. from the University of Tulsa College of Law. He sued the EPA fourteen times as Oklahoma's Attorney General. The record shows he sued the agency more than almost anyone before Trump nominated him to run it. He resigned in July 2018. The record shows fourteen federal ethics investigations open at that moment. Department of Health and Human Services: public health credentials. And vaccine court records. Obama picked Kathleen Sebelius for HHS. Sebelius has a B.A. from Trinity Washington University. The M.P.A. is from Kansas. She had served as Kansas Insurance Commissioner and as Governor of Kansas, jobs that put her inside healthcare markets, Medicaid, and public health infrastructure. His second HHS Secretary, Sylvia Mathews Burwell, held a Harvard B.A. and an M.Phil. in International Relations from Oxford. She ran the Global Development Program at the Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation. That was one of the largest global health funding portfolios on record. Then she moved to the Office of Management and Budget as Director. Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is Trump's 2025 HHS Secretary. He went to Harvard for undergrad and got his law degree at UVA. He practiced environmental law. That was his training. That was his practice. He holds no medical degree. His academic record lists no degree in public health, epidemiology, or any related field. His record shows no health system management, no public health agency leadership, no health policy authorship. He founded Children's Health Defense. Federal courts and public health researchers have documented it as a primary institutional source of vaccine misinformation. He now runs the agency that controls the CDC, the FDA, the National Institutes of Health, and Medicare and Medicaid. Attorney General: From Harvard Law to a Nomination That Could Not Survive Eric Holder was Obama's first Attorney General. He has an A.B. from Columbia University. J.D. from Columbia Law School. Federal prosecutor in the Public Integrity Section. Then a Superior Court judge in the District of Columbia. Then Deputy Attorney General under Clinton. He spent decades inside the institution before leading it. Lynch, Obama's second Attorney General, had an A.B. from Harvard College and a J.D. from Harvard Law. She served as United States Attorney for the Eastern District of New York. Twice. The district handles international organized crime, terrorism financing, and public corruption cases. Trump's 2025 nomination for Attorney General was Matt Gaetz , who holds a B.S. from the College of William and Mary and a J.D. from Florida State University. Three terms in the House. That's what the record shows. He had prosecuted no cases. He had not served as a state attorney general. He had not served as a federal one either. He had never led a law enforcement agency at any level. The nomination was withdrawn. No confirmation hearing was ever scheduled. The Credential Comparison: At a Glance If you want to boil it down to a single receipt, here it is. Department by department. Side by side. Documented credentials rated on a subject-matter qualification score: 0 means no relevant expertise, 5 means career-level domain mastery. Department Obama Appointee (DEI Era) Trump Appointee (Anti-DEI Era) The Gap Energy Ph.D. Physics (Nobel Prize winner) Steven Chu, MIT / Stanford 5/5 B.S. Animal Science; forgot the dept. existed Rick Perry, Texas A&M 1/5 Academic / research depth vs. executive credential only Education Ed.D. Columbia / Harvard; CEO of public schools John King & Arne Duncan 5/5 B.A. Business / WWE CEO; never attended a public school Betsy DeVos; Linda McMahon (no degree) 0/5 Zero public school experience vs. career educators EPA M.S. Chemical Engineering; 16 years at EPA Lisa Jackson, Princeton 5/5 J.D.; led 14 lawsuits against the EPA before leading it Scott Pruitt, Oklahoma AG 0/5 Scientific expertise vs. documented opposition to agency mission HUD M.Arch + M.P.A. Harvard; career in housing policy Shaun Donovan; Julian Castro 5/5 M.D. Pediatric Neurosurgery; no housing background Ben Carson, Yale / Johns Hopkins 2/5 Subject-matter alignment vs. prestigious but irrelevant credentials HHS M.P.A.; managed Gates Foundation global health portfolio Sylvia Burwell, Harvard / Oxford 5/5 J.D. Environmental Law; no public health credentials RFK Jr., Harvard / UVA Law 1/5 Health systems expertise vs. legal background in unrelated field Attorney General J.D. Harvard; federal prosecutor; Deputy AG Eric Holder; Loretta Lynch 5/5 J.D. FSU; 3 House terms; zero prosecutorial experience Matt Gaetz (withdrew before hearing) 1/5 Career prosecution experience vs. no law enforcement background The qualification score measures documented expertise in the agency's actual work: 0 is none, 5 is career-level mastery in that domain. Sources: Senate confirmation records, agency biographies, Nobel Committee archives, C-SPAN congressional archive. What the Pattern Shows The pattern across these comparisons is not a matter of party preference. It is a matter of documented record. The Obama administration, repeatedly characterized in 2025 as a DEI administration whose diversity commitments came at the expense of merit, consistently appointed people with deep subject-matter expertise to the agencies they led. A Nobel Prize-winning physicist to Energy. A career housing policy administrator to HUD. A sixteen-year EPA veteran to the EPA. Harvard Law prosecutors to the Justice Department. The Trump administration, which made the elimination of DEI a stated first-day priority on the grounds that DEI produces unqualified people, appointed an animal science graduate to Energy, a woman who had did not attend public school to Education, a surgeon with no housing policy background to HUD, the lead litigant against the EPA to lead the EPA, and a lawyer with no public health credentials to lead the public health apparatus of the United States government. This is not a subjective interpretation. These are the credentials. They are in the Senate confirmation records. They are in the university archives. They are on C-SPAN. The Question the Record Raises If the attack on DEI were actually about qualifications, the documented record would support it. It does not. The January 2025 removals included career civil servants holding advanced degrees in their fields, professionals with decades of subject-matter experience, and agency heads who had spent their careers in the work they were terminated from. The people appointed to replace institutional expertise with political alignment have, in multiple documented cases, less formal education in relevant fields, less professional experience in the agencies they lead, and in at least one case, a track record of active legal opposition to the agency's core mission. The attack on DEI was not, the record suggests, primarily about qualifications. The documented comparison makes that clear. What it was primarily about is a question this article does not answer. The record only shows what it was not about. It was not about qualifications.
